label: Direct and online preference optimization

node.label: Direct and online preference optimization
node.definition: Direct preference optimization and related objectives convert preferred–rejected response pairs into a policy update without an explicit online actor–critic loop.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Direct and online preference optimization is modeled as depending on Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning and feeding downstream nodes such as Large language models, Conversational assistants & chatbots.
examples: atlas downstream link to Large language models
atlas downstream link to Conversational assistants & chatbots
limitations: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Direct and online preference optimization has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) because Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

In this atlas graph, Direct and online preference optimization has a `uses` dependency on Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning because Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Direct and online preference optimization has a `uses` dependency on Preference learning because Preference learning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Direct and online preference optimization has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Process supervision & step-level reward models because Process supervision & step-level reward models is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
